title: Henry Patten's Scary Trip Home from Dubai
summary: Henry Patten is a tennis player from Britain who was in Dubai for a tennis tournament. While he was there, there was a conflict in the Middle East, and Iran attacked the United Arab Emirates with missiles. This made it hard for Henry and other people to go home. He saw a drone hit a building near where he was staying. Henry had to stay in Dubai longer than planned and tried to drive to Oman, but it didn't work. Finally, he took a flight back to London, but he was very scared because he doesn't like flying. On the plane, he felt nervous, but he made it home safely. This experience made him decide not to play in another tennis tournament in Indian Wells.

background_read: Henry Patten is a British tennis player who won a big tournament called the Australian Open. He was in Dubai for a tennis event when a conflict between Iran and the United Arab Emirates started. This conflict involved missiles and drones, making it unsafe. Many people, including tennis players, got stuck and couldn't leave easily. Henry had to find a way home, which was scary for him because he is afraid of flying. This story shows how sports can be affected by world events.
Article_Structure: WHO: Henry Patten, a British tennis player
WHAT: He experienced a conflict in Dubai, saw a drone attack, and had a scary flight home
WHERE: Dubai in the United Arab Emirates and on a flight to London
WHY: Because there was a Middle East conflict that made travel difficult and dangerous

perspectives.description: He felt scared and anxious during the conflict and flight, showing how personal fears can mix with global events.
